note: SEOOC HARA. go-CAN is a Safety Element Out Of Context; the operational situations and ASIL ratings are assumptions on the integrating item, to be confirmed by the integrator's system-level HARA. See SAFETY_PLAN.md §1 and SEOOC assumptions (REQ-SEOOC-*).

operationalSituations.description: Vehicle in motion; ECU exchanging periodic safety-relevant signals over CAN (e.g. powertrain/chassis state).
Vehicle stationary in service mode; diagnostic tester performing UDS/OBD-II read/write or ECU reset.
Multi-frame diagnostic transfer in progress over ISO-TP (e.g. VIN read, calibration download).

hazards.description: Undetected corruption of a received frame payload is delivered to the application as valid data.
A safety-relevant frame is silently dropped or lost, so the application acts on stale data without notification.
A DBC signal is mis-decoded or mis-encoded (wrong scaling, byte order, or bit truncation), yielding an incorrect physical value.
ISO-TP reassembly accepts out-of-order, overlapping, or oversized segments and produces an incorrect or incomplete diagnostic message.
A frame is transmitted with an out-of-range or malformed arbitration ID / DLC, corrupting another node's reception or violating bus framing.

safetyGoals.description: The library shall not present corrupted frame data as valid; data integrity must be verifiable end-to-end (E2E protection header).
safetyGoals.safeState: Application is signalled (via E2E CRC/counter mismatch) and discards the frame.

safetyGoals.description: Frame loss caused by back-pressure shall be observable; drops shall be counted and the policy explicit, never silent under a Block contract.
safetyGoals.safeState: Drop counters increment and are exposed via MetricsProvider for the application to detect.

safetyGoals.description: DBC encode/decode shall be the exact inverse over the signal's representable range, with no silent truncation or wrap.
safetyGoals.safeState: Out-of-range physical values are clamped deterministically; round-trip is verified.

safetyGoals.description: ISO-TP reassembly shall reject any frame that violates the segmentation protocol (sequence number, length, type).
safetyGoals.safeState: Reassembly aborts with an error; no partial message is delivered.

safetyGoals.description: Every transmitted frame shall be validated against CAN/CAN-FD structural constraints before it reaches the bus.
safetyGoals.safeState: Invalid frames are rejected at Send with ErrInvalidFrame; nothing is transmitted.
